figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polyline points='20,30 70,30 70,60 120,60 120,90 170,90 170,120 200,120' fill='none' stroke='#92400e' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='70' y='55' width='50' height='5' fill='#16a34a'/><text x='74' y='52' font-size='7' fill='#16a34a'>catch berm</text></svg>

options: catch falling rock and improve wall safety
increase the overall slope angle
act as the main haul road
store blasting explosives
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Catch (safety) berms intercept rockfall from upper faces.

**Reasoning.** The berm width is designed to retain rock that ravels off the bench faces above, protecting work areas below. It actually flattens (not steepens) the overall slope.

**Answer.** Catch falling rock and improve wall safety.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: From the cross-section shown (overburden 300 m², coal 100 m² per metre strike), compute the instantaneous stripping ratio.

figure.caption: Section at current face
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='30' y='30' width='180' height='60' fill='#d6d3d1' stroke='#475569'/><text x='120' y='65' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>overburden 300 m^2</text><rect x='30' y='90' width='180' height='40' fill='#1f2937'/><text x='120' y='115' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle' fill='#fff'>coal 100 m^2</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Instantaneous SR is the local overburden-to-coal area ratio.

**Working.**
$$SR = \frac{300}{100} = 3$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{3}$ (3:1).

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A bench face is 15 m high with a horizontal toe-to-crest offset of 8.66 m as shown. Compute the bench face angle (degrees).

figure.caption: Bench face
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='40,30 40,120 100,120' fill='#fde9c8' stroke='#92400e'/><text x='22' y='80' font-size='8' transform='rotate(-90 24 80)'>H=15</text><text x='52' y='135' font-size='8'>horiz 8.66</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The face angle is $\theta = \tan^{-1}(H/\text{offset})$.

**Working.**
$$\theta = \tan^{-1}\!\left(\frac{15}{8.66}\right) = \tan^{-1}(1.732) = 60^\circ$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{60^\circ}$.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: An open pit must remove $8\ \text{Mm}^3$ of waste to win $2\ \text{Mm}^3$ of ore (figure). Compute the overall stripping ratio.

figure.caption: Waste over ore cross-section
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='20,40 220,40 180,90 60,90' fill='#cbd5e1' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='80' y='90' width='80' height='30' fill='#92400e' stroke='#475569'/><text x='88' y='70' font-size='8'>waste 8 Mm^3</text><text x='98' y='110' font-size='8' fill='#ffffff'>ore 2 Mm^3</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Stripping ratio $=\dfrac{\text{waste}}{\text{ore}}$.

**Working.**
$$SR=\frac{8}{2}=4$$

**Answer.** $4$.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A bench block is $200\ \text{m}$ long, $30\ \text{m}$ wide and $15\ \text{m}$ high (figure). Compute the in-place volume ($\mathrm{m^3}$).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $V=L\times W\times H$.

**Working.**
$$V=200\times30\times15=90000\ \mathrm{m^3}$$

**Answer.** $90000\ \mathrm{m^3}$.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A shovel with a $5\ \mathrm{m^3}$ bucket completes $120$ cycles per hour at a fill factor of $0.9$ (figure). Compute the hourly output ($\mathrm{m^3/hr}$).

figure.caption: Power shovel
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='40' y='80' width='50' height='30' fill='#94a3b8' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='90' y1='90' x2='140' y2='60' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='3'/><path d='M140 60 L160 70 L150 90 L135 80 Z' fill='#6b7280' stroke='#475569'/><text x='25' y='135' font-size='8'>bucket 5 m^3, 120 cycles/hr</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Output $=$ bucket $\times$ cycles $\times$ fill factor.

**Working.**
$$Q=5\times120\times0.9=540\ \mathrm{m^3/hr}$$

**Answer.** $540\ \mathrm{m^3/hr}$.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A haul truck cycle comprises loading $2\ \text{min}$, hauling $5\ \text{min}$, dumping $1\ \text{min}$ and returning $4\ \text{min}$ (figure). Compute the cycle time (min).

figure.caption: Truck haulage cycle
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='100' cy='75' r='50' fill='none' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='100,25 108,33 100,41 92,33' fill='#dc2626'/><text x='58' y='80' font-size='8'>load+haul+</text><text x='60' y='95' font-size='8'>dump+return</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Cycle time is the sum of the components.

**Working.**
$$t=2+5+1+4=12\ \text{min}$$

**Answer.** $12$ min.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A shovel loads one truck every $3\ \text{min}$ and each truck has a cycle time of $12\ \text{min}$ (figure). Compute the number of trucks needed to keep the shovel fully occupied.

figure.caption: Truck-shovel matching
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='50' width='40' height='25' fill='#94a3b8' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><rect x='90' y='50' width='25' height='18'/><rect x='125' y='50' width='25' height='18'/><rect x='160' y='50' width='25' height='18'/></g><text x='25' y='100' font-size='8'>1 shovel, fleet of trucks</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Trucks $=\dfrac{\text{cycle time}}{\text{loading interval}}$.

**Working.**
$$N=\frac{12}{3}=4$$

**Answer.** $4$ trucks.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A haul road rises $30\ \text{m}$ over a horizontal distance of $300\ \text{m}$ (figure). Compute the road gradient (%).

figure.caption: Haul road gradient
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='30,100 200,100 200,40' fill='#d6d3d1' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='200' y1='100' x2='200' y2='40' stroke='#dc2626' marker-end='url(#g)' marker-start='url(#g)'/><line x1='30' y1='112' x2='200' y2='112' stroke='#2563eb'/><defs><marker id='g' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='3' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#475569'/></marker></defs><text x='205' y='75' font-size='8'>30</text><text x='90' y='125' font-size='8'>run 300</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Gradient $=\dfrac{\text{rise}}{\text{run}}\times100$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{30}{300}\times100=10\%$$

**Answer.** $10\%$.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A rear-dump truck carries $10\ \mathrm{m^3}$ of rock with a loose density of $2\ \mathrm{t/m^3}$ (figure). Compute the payload (t).

figure.caption: Loaded dump truck
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='40,50 130,50 140,80 30,80' fill='#94a3b8' stroke='#475569'/><circle cx='55' cy='95' r='12' fill='#1f2937'/><circle cx='115' cy='95' r='12' fill='#1f2937'/><text x='28' y='120' font-size='8'>10 m^3 at 2 t/m^3</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Payload $=$ volume $\times$ density.

**Working.**
$$W=10\times2=20\ \text{t}$$

**Answer.** $20$ t.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: An overall pit slope rises $H=120\ \text{m}$ over a horizontal projection of $80\ \text{m}$ (figure). Compute the overall slope angle (degrees, two decimals).

figure.caption: Overall pit slope
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='30,30 30,120 190,120' fill='#d6d3d1' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='30' x2='190' y2='120' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2'/><text x='35' y='70' font-size='8'>H=120</text><text x='95' y='133' font-size='8'>horizontal 80</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\theta=\tan^{-1}\!\left(\dfrac{H}{\text{horizontal}}\right)$.

**Working.**
$$\theta=\tan^{-1}\!\left(\frac{120}{80}\right)=\tan^{-1}(1.5)=56.31^\circ$$

**Answer.** $56.31^\circ$.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: The narrow horizontal shelf left on the pit slope (highlighted in the figure) is a:

figure.caption: Catch berm on a slope
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='20,30 80,30 80,60 140,60 140,90 200,90 200,130 20,130' fill='#d6d3d1' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='80' y='52' width='60' height='8' fill='#16a34a'/><text x='30' y='145' font-size='8'>shelf on the bench face</text></svg>

options: safety (catch) berm to retain falling rock and provide access
ramp for haul trucks to climb the pit
sump for collecting pit water
stockpile for blended ore
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Pit slopes are stepped with benches and berms.

**Reasoning.** The horizontal shelf on the bench face catches rockfall, limits run-out and gives access; it is the safety or catch berm, distinct from the inclined haul ramp.

**Answer.** Safety (catch) berm to retain falling rock and provide access.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: The machine shown casts overburden by dragging a bucket on ropes toward itself. It is a:

figure.caption: Dragline boom and bucket
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='30' y='90' width='40' height='25' fill='#94a3b8' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='60' y1='95' x2='170' y2='40' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='3'/><line x1='170' y1='40' x2='160' y2='100' stroke='#6b7280'/><path d='M150 100 L170 100 L165 115 L148 112 Z' fill='#6b7280' stroke='#475569'/><text x='28' y='140' font-size='8'>boom and dragged bucket</text></svg>

options: dragline that digs below its standing level
front-end loader that digs above grade only
continuous bucket-wheel excavator
hydraulic breaker
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Stripping equipment classification.

**Reasoning.** A dragline suspends its bucket on ropes from a long boom and drags it inward, excavating below the machine's standing level to cast overburden to a spoil side.

**Answer.** Dragline that digs below its standing level.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A bench of $90000\ \mathrm{m^3}$ is removed over $30\ \text{working days}$ (figure). Compute the average daily production ($\mathrm{m^3/day}$).

figure.caption: Production over time
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='100' x2='200' y2='100' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='100' x2='30' y2='20' stroke='#475569'/><polyline points='30,90 80,70 130,50 180,30' fill='none' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><text x='40' y='120' font-size='8'>90000 m^3 in 30 days</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Daily rate $=\dfrac{\text{volume}}{\text{days}}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{90000}{30}=3000\ \mathrm{m^3/day}$$

**Answer.** $3000\ \mathrm{m^3/day}$.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: In the loader-and-fleet arrangement shown, the fleet is best matched to the loader when:

figure.caption: Loader with truck fleet
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='50' width='35' height='22' fill='#94a3b8' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><rect x='80' y='52' width='22' height='16'/><rect x='112' y='52' width='22' height='16'/><rect x='144' y='52' width='22' height='16'/><rect x='176' y='52' width='22' height='16'/></g><text x='25' y='100' font-size='8'>matching trucks to loader</text></svg>

options: the trucks arrive just as the loader finishes the previous one, minimising waiting on both sides
as many trucks as possible queue so the loader never idles, regardless of truck waiting
only one truck is used to simplify scheduling
the loader waits idle most of the shift
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Fleet balance minimises total queuing and idle costs.

**Reasoning.** The optimum match has trucks arriving as the loader becomes free, so neither the loader idles nor the trucks queue excessively; too few trucks idle the loader, too many waste truck time.

**Answer.** The trucks arrive just as the loader finishes the previous one, minimising waiting on both sides.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A bank volume of $100\ \mathrm{m^3}$ of rock has a swell of $25\%$ when excavated (figure). Compute the loose (in-truck) volume ($\mathrm{m^3}$).

figure.caption: Bank versus loose volume
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='30' y='60' width='60' height='60' fill='#a8a29e' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='130' y='40' width='70' height='80' fill='#cbd5e1' stroke='#475569'/><text x='30' y='135' font-size='8'>bank 100 m^3</text><text x='130' y='135' font-size='8'>loose +25%</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Loose volume $=$ bank volume $\times(1+\text{swell})$.

**Working.**
$$V_L=100\times1.25=125\ \mathrm{m^3}$$

**Answer.** $125\ \mathrm{m^3}$.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: The excavator shown (a rotating wheel of buckets feeding a conveyor) is classified as:

figure.caption: Bucket-wheel excavator
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='60' cy='70' r='30' fill='none' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><g fill='#6b7280'><rect x='56' y='38' width='8' height='8'/><rect x='86' y='66' width='8' height='8'/><rect x='56' y='94' width='8' height='8'/><rect x='26' y='66' width='8' height='8'/></g><line x1='90' y1='80' x2='190' y2='110' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='6'/><text x='30' y='140' font-size='8'>wheel of buckets and conveyor</text></svg>

options: a continuous excavator that digs and conveys without a load-haul cycle
a cyclic excavator that loads discrete buckets into trucks
a drilling machine
a crushing plant
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Surface excavators are cyclic or continuous.

**Reasoning.** A bucket-wheel excavator digs continuously as the wheel rotates and discharges onto a conveyor, giving a steady output stream rather than discrete load-haul-dump cycles.

**Answer.** A continuous excavator that digs and conveys without a load-haul cycle.
